operating_state: First quarter 2025 earnings of $7.7 billion decreased from $8.2 billion driven by weaker industry refining margins normalizing from historically high levels and lower crude prices. Upstream production increased 767 thousand oil-equivalent barrels daily to 4.6 million barrels from Pioneer acquisition and growing Permian and Guyana volumes. Energy Products earnings declined substantially due to margin compression while Chemical Products earnings fell on higher North American feed costs and reduced opportunistic sales volumes.
cost_structure: Structural cost savings contributed $0.6 billion bringing cumulative savings to $12.7 billion versus 2019 levels through operational efficiencies and workforce reductions. Cash operating expenses excluding energy and production taxes increased reflecting higher activity levels and growth investments. Depreciation and depletion expenses rose from increased asset base from acquisitions and new projects.
material_events: The company repurchased $4.8 billion of shares representing 43.4 million shares as part of ongoing buyback program. Total debt decreased to $37.6 billion from $41.7 billion at year-end 2024, reducing debt-to-capital ratio to 12.2 percent. The company issued floating rate notes due 2075 in March 2025.
macro_exposures: The U.S. announced various trade-related actions including tariffs on imports from several countries with many countries announcing retaliatory tariffs in response. Certain tariffs were paused but not withdrawn creating continued volatility in the global trade environment. The likelihood of resuming tariffs, imposing new reciprocal tariffs, export restrictions, or other trade-related sanctions remains highly uncertain with unclear effects on the Corporation, suppliers, customers, and overall macroeconomic environment.
forward_outlook: The Corporation plans capital investments of $27 billion to $29 billion for 2025 with actual spending dependent on project progress and property acquisitions. Management maintains capital allocation priorities of investing in competitively advantaged high-return projects, maintaining strong balance sheet, and consistent shareholder returns through share repurchases and growing dividends. The company continues evaluating asset portfolio optimization through ongoing divestment programs and potential acquisitions based on strategic fit and attractive valuations.
uncertainty_profile: Forward-looking statements regarding emission reduction plans including net zero targets by 2050 and Permian Basin goals by 2030-2035 depend on technological progress, stable policy support, and timely permitting. Environmental sustainability statements are based on evolving measurement standards and assumptions subject to future change including regulatory rule-making. Project advancement depends on stable policy frameworks, permitting approvals, technological cost-effectiveness, and stakeholder alignment with investment levels subject to opportunity availability and public policy support.
